factors.name: Tech Equity Valuation & AI Skepticism
factors.summary: Jeremy Grantham calls U.S. stock market 'most expensive in American history,' citing AI-driven valuations. Tech slump deepened this week as investors reassessed sustainability of the AI trade and rising semiconductor/memory costs.

factors.name: Memory Chip Supply Crunch
factors.summary: Memory shortage described as 'existential crisis' for smaller consumer electronics players; Apple and Microsoft raising prices to cover soaring memory costs, suggesting margin pressure ahead for tech mega-caps.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: U.S. conducting fresh strikes on Iran after tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz, escalating despite supposed 60-day ceasefire. Ship traffic in Hormuz down from 140/day (Feb) to 30-40 currently, creating supply-chain and energy volatility risk.

factors.name: Inflation Upside Risks Emerging
factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel warns of upside inflation risks despite peace deal optimism. Europe heatwave (110°F+ in France) raising climate inflation concerns; crude oil weakness offering limited offset to cost pressures.

factors.name: AI Capex Burden & Energy Crunch
factors.summary: AI's energy crunch driving Wall Street bets on power-solution IPO winners; GE Vernova turbines powering data centers signals massive capex requirements. AI bubble narrative intensifying amid skepticism on ROI.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Momentum (NAS-Specific Positive)
factors.summary: SpaceX joining Nasdaq-100 via fast-tracked process will drive massive ETF buying demand and boost Elon Musk ecosystem sentiment; immediate liquidity tail wind for NDX.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness & Risk-Off Tone
factors.summary: Bitcoin holding above $60K but facing capitulation risk; 50K BTC moved at a loss, short-term holders' stress at 2-year highs. Crypto equities (Coinbase, Circle) underperforming Big Tech; CZ blames 2026 crypto slump on AI, global tension, 4-year cycle.

factors.name: Bond-Equity Correlation at 30-Year Low
factors.summary: Decoupling of bonds and equities creates ambiguity for traditional hedges; tech's duration sensitivity to real yields less predictable in low-correlation regime.

factors.name: Recent Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: NDX down 0.75% in 4h, -0.58% in 24h—modest moves but directionally consistent with tech underperformance narrative. Move is early-phase but gaining conviction.

factors.name: FedEx Demand Signal (Positive Surprise)
factors.summary: FedEx CFO stated 'demand destruction concerns have not at all been the case' in latest quarter; freight demand remains robust, suggesting economic resilience and growth continuation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between conflicting signals: AI capex exuberance has stalled amid valuation skepticism (Grantham) and rising semiconductor/memory costs threatening margins, while geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strikes) and emerging inflation risks (ECB hawkishness, European heatwave) weigh on growth multiples. SpaceX's fast-track Nasdaq inclusion offers a near-term liquidity tailwind, but the broader tech correction narrative is reasserting dominance as investors reassess AI ROI and real yields remain elevated. FedEx's robust demand data provides a solitary economic green light, insufficient to offset the confluence of bearish factors.
catalysts_ahead: Q2 mega-cap earnings revisions (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA guidance on capex & AI demand next 2–4 weeks)
Next CPI/PCE release (inflation surprise would reset Fed rate expectations and real yields)
FOMC speakers or rate expectations shift (any hawkish surprise = NQ downside)
Geopolitical escalation or de-escalation in Strait of Hormuz (crude oil volatility → inflation expectations → growth multiple impact)
Semiconductor supply/memory pricing updates (TSMC, ASML earnings/commentary)
SpaceX Nasdaq debut and IPO concession/short squeeze (near-term tailwind, but likely 1-2 week event)
screening.risk_warning: If FedEx-like demand signals broaden (strong Amazon Prime Day results, incremental big-tech capex commits, Fed pivot to dovish tone), the bearish AI-skepticism narrative could flip to BULLISH quickly. Also, if Iran tensions de-escalate and Strait of Hormuz reopens, crude oil rally could ease inflation fears and extend tech multiple expansion. Real yields remain the pivot—any 10Y TIPS dip would aggressively support NQ. Watch for tactical short squeezes into SpaceX IPO hype.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is SELL but move is early-phase and modest (-0.75% in 4h); wait for intraday retest of 29300–29400 resistance before considering short entries. If 29500 breaks with volume, re-assess to NEUTRAL. For longs, any dip to 28850–28950 support on light volume offers a tactical bounce trade but lacks conviction given valuation and geopolitical headwinds.
